source.title: A neostriatal habit learning system in humans.

confidence_rationale: Canonical, much-replicated double dissociation in matched neurological patient groups. Source quality is high (Science, controlled clinical comparison, foundational paper); mapping fidelity is strong because the architectural separation REE asserts (model-free habit vs declarative attribution) is the same separation Knowlton operationalised. Transfer risk is moderate -- amnesic group is mostly diencephalic/medial-temporal lesions rather than AD specifically, so AD-relevance is by inference not direct measurement.
failure_signatures: If REE's architectural separation were wrong, amnesics would also be impaired on probabilistic classification (they were not).
If habit learning required a hippocampal/cortical consolidation pipeline, Parkinson patients with intact MTL would still acquire it (they did not).

mapping.source_claim_statement: Amnesic patients show normal probabilistic classification learning despite impaired declarative memory; Parkinson patients show the inverse pattern. The neostriatum supports a habit-learning system that is parallel to and independent of the limbic-diencephalic declarative system.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-172 predicts exactly this dissociation: the model-free habit system (MECH-163, D1/D2 SNc loops) does not depend on the hippocampal/E3 attribution pipeline, so degradation of one leaves the other intact. Knowlton's double dissociation is the canonical demonstration of the architectural separation REE invokes.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Knowlton's amnesic group was diencephalic + medial-temporal (Korsakoff, anoxia), not AD specifically. Knowlton's BG-disorder group was Parkinson, not Huntington. The AD/HD framing in MECH-172 is one step removed from Knowlton's own patient groups; the inference rests on AD's hippocampal/temporal damage profile being more amnesia-like and HD's striatal damage being more Parkinson-like.
mapping.source_context: Adult neurological patients (amnesics with diencephalic or MTL damage; Parkinson patients without dementia) and matched controls; probabilistic classification (weather-prediction) task with declarative-memory probe.

confidence_components.notes: Foundational and replicated across many follow-ups; source quality near ceiling. Mapping is high but indirect for the AD-vs-HD framing of MECH-172.
